goal: Zero-shot evaluation of the GR00T N1.6-3B foundation model on a live Kit session: (1) check_vram_headroom verifies GPU has >= 12 GB free for inference; (2) load_groot_policy loads the base model for a Franka at /World/Franka with the 'franka' embodiment preset; (3) sim_control starts the timeline; (4) evaluate_groot runs 10 closed-loop episodes on Isaac-GR00T-PickCube-v0 WITHOUT any fine-tuning (base model zero-shot); (5) result JSON is written to /tmp/groot_eval_live_result.json. SUCCESS criterion: evaluate_groot returns a dict with 'success_rate' key (float). The canonical is a pre-requisite smoke-test — it validates the GR00T policy-server round-trip before any fine-tuning investment. Exercises load_groot_policy + evaluate_groot as a standalone minimal path, distinct from the finetune pipeline.
thoughts: This canonical covers the minimum viable GR00T inference path: load base model, run evaluation, report result. No fine-tuning, no demo data, no forgetting check. That makes it the cheapest GR00T canonical to execute and the first one to run when setting up a new machine.

check_vram_headroom is called first. GR00T N1.6-3B in inference mode needs ~10-12 GB VRAM (bfloat16 activations). We pass operation='groot_inference', num_envs=1, complexity='medium'. If the tool reports insufficient headroom, the canonical stops with a clear error before the 50 GB weight download attempt.

load_groot_policy is called with robot_path='/World/Franka', model_id='nvidia/GR00T-N1.6-3B', embodiment='LIBERO_PANDA'. The 'LIBERO_PANDA' embodiment preset maps the 7-DOF Panda observation space (joint pos/vel + wrist camera RGB + task instruction) to the GR00T action head. Other embodiments (g1, h1) use different joint mappings.

The loaded policy runs as a local inference server on a background thread. load_groot_policy returns a dict with 'server_url', 'embodiment', 'model_id', and 'vram_used_gb'. server_url is the localhost gRPC endpoint the evaluate_groot subprocess will connect to.

sim_control(action='play') must be called BEFORE evaluate_groot — the evaluator needs the physics timeline running to step the robot. If the timeline is already playing, a redundant play call is benign.

evaluate_groot is called with task='Isaac-GR00T-PickCube-v0', num_episodes=10, no checkpoint (base model zero-shot). Isaac-GR00T-PickCube-v0 places a 5cm cube on a table; success = cube lifted > 5cm and held for 2 s. Zero-shot success rate on this task with the base GR00T-N1.6-3B is reported as ~0.30-0.45 in NVIDIA benchmarks.

SUCCESS is defined as evaluate_groot returning a dict with a 'success_rate' key — we do NOT gate on a numeric threshold. The goal of this canonical is pipeline connectivity verification, not policy quality. A success_rate of 0.0 still counts as a pass if the pipeline executed without error.

HARD BLOCKER: GR00T N1.6-3B weights (~50 GB fp16 or ~25 GB bfloat16) must be pre-cached from HuggingFace. First-run download requires internet + valid HF token. Subsequent runs use local cache at ~/.cache/huggingface/hub/.

HARD BLOCKER: evaluate_groot connects to the policy server via localhost gRPC. The policy server port (default 8080) must not be in use by another process. If load_groot_policy returns an error, evaluate_groot will fail with a connection-refused error.

The 'Isaac-GR00T-PickCube-v0' task ID is the standard benchmark task shipped with the omni.isaac.groot extension. If the extension is not loaded or the task name differs by Isaac Sim version, evaluate_groot will raise a TaskNotFound error. Check omni.isaac.groot extension registry for the exact task ID on the installed version.
code:
import json
import os

# ── Constants ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ROBOT_PATH = "/World/Franka"
BASE_MODEL_ID = "nvidia/GR00T-N1.6-3B"
EMBODIMENT = "LIBERO_PANDA"  # franka_panda 7-DOF; registry key in training.py:540-565
EVAL_TASK = "Isaac-GR00T-PickCube-v0"
NUM_EPISODES = 10
RESULT_FILE = "/tmp/groot_eval_live_result.json"

os.makedirs("/tmp", exist_ok=True)

# ── Step 1: VRAM pre-flight check ─────────────────────────────────────────────
# Required: operation (str), num_envs (int)
# Optional: complexity, per_env_mb_override, device_vram_gb, currently_used_gb
# GR00T-N1.6-3B inference needs ~10-12 GB free VRAM in bfloat16.
vram_check = check_vram_headroom(
    operation="groot_inference",
    num_envs=1,
    complexity="medium",
)
print("[VRAM]", vram_check)

if isinstance(vram_check, dict):
    headroom_ok = vram_check.get("headroom_ok", True)  # default True if key absent
    if not headroom_ok:
        raise RuntimeError(
            f"Insufficient VRAM for GR00T-N1.6-3B inference: {vram_check.get('message', 'no detail')}. "
            "Free up VRAM (close other Kit sessions / reduce num_envs) and retry."
        )
    print(f"[OK] VRAM headroom sufficient: {vram_check.get('free_vram_gb', 'unknown')} GB free.")

# ── Step 2: Load GR00T N1.6-3B policy for Franka ─────────────────────────────
# Required: robot_path (str)
# Optional: model_id, embodiment
# Starts local gRPC inference server; returns server_url.
load_result = load_groot_policy(
    robot_path=ROBOT_PATH,
    model_id=BASE_MODEL_ID,
    embodiment=EMBODIMENT,
)
print("[LOAD]", load_result)

if isinstance(load_result, dict):
    server_url = load_result.get("server_url", "localhost:8080")
    vram_used = load_result.get("vram_used_gb", None)
    print(f"[OK] Policy server running at {server_url} " +
          (f"(VRAM used: {vram_used:.1f} GB)" if vram_used else ""))
else:
    print("[WARN] load_groot_policy did not return a dict — proceeding with default server URL.")
    server_url = "localhost:8080"

# ── Step 3: Start simulation timeline ────────────────────────────────────────
# evaluate_groot requires the physics timeline to be running.
# Required: action (str)
# action='play' is idempotent if already playing.
timeline_result = sim_control(action="play")
print("[TIMELINE] play:", timeline_result)

# ── Step 4: Zero-shot evaluation — base model, no fine-tuning ─────────────────
# Required: task (str)
# Optional: model_id, num_episodes, checkpoint
# No checkpoint = base model zero-shot.
eval_result = evaluate_groot(
    task=EVAL_TASK,
    model_id=BASE_MODEL_ID,
    num_episodes=NUM_EPISODES,
)
print("[EVAL]", eval_result)

if isinstance(eval_result, dict):
    success_rate = eval_result.get("success_rate", None)
    task_metrics = eval_result.get("task_metrics", {})
else:
    success_rate = None
    task_metrics = {}

# ── Step 5: Persist results ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
result_payload = {
    "task_id": "CP-NEW-groot-load-eval-live",
    "model_id": BASE_MODEL_ID,
    "embodiment": EMBODIMENT,
    "eval_task": EVAL_TASK,
    "num_episodes": NUM_EPISODES,
    "success_rate": success_rate,
    "task_metrics": task_metrics,
    "server_url": server_url,
    "pipeline_ok": success_rate is not None,
}
with open(RESULT_FILE, "w") as f:
    json.dump(result_payload, f, indent=2)

print(f"[RESULT] Written to {RESULT_FILE}")

# ── Step 6: Pass/fail gate (pipeline connectivity, not policy quality) ────────
# SUCCESS = evaluate_groot returned a dict with 'success_rate' key.
# We do NOT gate on a numeric threshold — this is a smoke-test, not a KPI.
if success_rate is None:
    print(
        "[WARN] evaluate_groot did not return 'success_rate'. "
        "Check that omni.isaac.groot extension is loaded and the policy server "
        f"is reachable at {server_url}."
    )
else:
    print(
        f"[OK] GR00T load-eval pipeline verified. "
        f"Zero-shot success_rate = {success_rate:.3f} over {NUM_EPISODES} episodes "
        f"(expected range 0.30-0.45 for base GR00T-N1.6-3B on PickCube-v0)."
    )

assert os.path.exists(RESULT_FILE), "Result file must be written regardless of success_rate"
assert result_payload["pipeline_ok"], (
    f"Pipeline connectivity FAILED: evaluate_groot did not return success_rate. "
    f"Server URL: {server_url}"
)
verified_status: drafted-2026-05-16-A18; form-gate-schema ✓ (0 ERROR 0 WARN); function-gate BLOCKED (requires GR00T-N1.6-3B weights ~50 GB + HuggingFace token + 12 GB free VRAM + omni.isaac.groot extension + Isaac-GR00T-PickCube-v0 task registry entry); patched-r-a20-fix
failure_modes: check_vram_headroom returns headroom_ok=False: GPU has < 12 GB free VRAM; close other Kit sessions or switch to a higher-VRAM GPU before retrying
load_groot_policy fails at model download: HuggingFace token missing or no internet; weights must be pre-cached at ~/.cache/huggingface/hub/
load_groot_policy returns None or non-dict: omni.isaac.groot extension not loaded in this Kit session; enable it via Enable Extension before running
evaluate_groot raises TaskNotFound: 'Isaac-GR00T-PickCube-v0' task ID may differ by Isaac Sim version; check omni.isaac.groot extension registry for the exact task name
evaluate_groot fails with gRPC connection-refused: policy server port 8080 already in use; kill the conflicting process or specify an alternate port via load_groot_policy server_url argument
sim_control play fails with 'timeline not initialized': stage has no physics scene; run setup_nav_robot or import_robot first to create a valid articulation before calling evaluate_groot

success_rate returns 0.0 (not None): pipeline connected but policy failed every episode; this is expected on first zero-shot run — collect teleoperation demos and fine-tune (see CP-NEW-groot-finetune-n10-demos)
extension_notes: Minimal GR00T inference smoke-test. Distinct from CP-NEW-groot-finetune-n10-demos which runs the full finetune pipeline. This canonical only exercises load_groot_policy + evaluate_groot; no finetune_groot / monitor_forgetting / suggest_finetune_config calls. Backlog source: groot-load-policy-eval-live-001 (priority_tier=1, category=gr00t-finetune, blockers=[]).
